required_doc_refs.why: Defines the GAP-130 through GAP-139 execution queue and the GAP-131 classifier requirement.
Captures the refreshed borrowing review that the classifier turns into executable adoption decisions.
Defines the human-readable semantics for this classifier artifact.

entries.title: Codex host surface
entries.summary: Codex remains a primary cooperation host rather than a capability to replace.

effect_hypothesis.expected_benefit: Preserves the native host path while later governance capabilities stay subordinate.
effect_hypothesis.expected_cost: Requires explicit compatibility tracking when new governed controls appear.
effect_hypothesis.expected_risk: Host-specific assumptions may drift if current-source compatibility is not refreshed.
effect_hypothesis.effect_metric: Current-source compatibility and host parity evidence remain green.

entries.rollback: Revert only the classifier entry and evidence update if the host-boundary statement is proven inaccurate.

entries.title: Claude Code provider surface
entries.summary: Claude Code remains a primary cooperation host and local third-party-provider usage surface.

effect_hypothesis.expected_benefit: Keeps the repository aligned with the owner-corrected product boundary.
effect_hypothesis.expected_cost: Requires provider-boundary language to stay precise in docs and future control packs.
effect_hypothesis.expected_risk: Official-subscription assumptions could reappear in docs or generated artifacts.
effect_hypothesis.effect_metric: Host-replacement claim scans and current-source compatibility checks stay green.

entries.rollback: Restore the prior wording if the provider boundary is later redefined by project evidence.

entries.title: MCP adapter boundary
entries.summary: Retain MCP as a governed adapter surface but improve classification before control packs are materialized.

effect_hypothesis.expected_benefit: Clarifies that adapter reuse must become an executable control, not only a research note.
effect_hypothesis.expected_cost: Requires later control-pack typing and inheritance rules.
effect_hypothesis.expected_risk: Boundary blur may survive until GAP-132 and GAP-133 land.
effect_hypothesis.effect_metric: Later control-pack contract validation can point back to this classifier entry.

entries.rollback: Drop the improvement classification if MCP adapter boundaries are removed from the active queue.

entries.title: OPA policy boundary
entries.summary: Treat full OPA runtime adoption as a deferred reference instead of a near-term implementation target.

effect_hypothesis.expected_benefit: Prevents premature policy-engine breadth from blocking the governed runtime path.
effect_hypothesis.expected_cost: Keeps some policy-language aspirations in documentation only for now.
effect_hypothesis.expected_risk: Future readers may misread deprecation as permanent exclusion without a trigger.
effect_hypothesis.effect_metric: Plan and backlog continue to defer full OPA runtime adoption without drift.

entries.rollback: Reclassify to improve or add if a future queue explicitly funds executable OPA integration.
entries.retention_rule: Keep only the research boundary and do not create an active implementation claim before later effect evidence exists.
entries.future_trigger: Promote only after a target repo needs executable cross-host policy evaluation with measurable effect.

entries.title: LangGraph workflow vocabulary
entries.summary: Absorb workflow vocabulary selectively as naming and contract guidance for later control-pack orchestration fields.

effect_hypothesis.expected_benefit: Lets later workflow contracts use established orchestration terminology without adopting a full workflow service.
effect_hypothesis.expected_cost: Requires disciplined scope control so vocabulary does not imply a platform commitment.
effect_hypothesis.expected_risk: Terminology could outpace runtime support if not tied to executable controls.
effect_hypothesis.effect_metric: GAP-132 control-pack fields can cite workflow vocabulary without adding unsupported runtime claims.

entries.rollback: Remove the vocabulary entry if later control-pack design chooses a simpler repository-native naming scheme.

entries.title: OpenHands execution boundary
entries.summary: Retire full OpenHands-like execution-host ambitions from the active near-term queue.

effect_hypothesis.expected_benefit: Keeps the repository focused on governed controls rather than building a competing execution host.
effect_hypothesis.expected_cost: Some long-range execution-host ideas stay in historical research only.
effect_hypothesis.expected_risk: Readers may still infer host-competition intent from older documents.
effect_hypothesis.effect_metric: Current working-set docs keep host cooperation wording and do not reopen execution-host product scope.

entries.rollback: Create a new funded queue before restoring execution-host ambitions to active scope.
entries.retention_rule: Keep historical research only; do not promote into active queue items without a new scope decision.
entries.future_trigger: Reopen only if the owner explicitly asks for a first-party execution host with separate effect metrics.

entries.title: SWE-agent repair loop
entries.summary: Improve bugfix-loop borrowing criteria before any self-evolution flow can adopt repair-loop behavior.

effect_hypothesis.expected_benefit: Keeps repair-loop borrowing tied to evidence, rollback, and target-repo effect metrics.
effect_hypothesis.expected_cost: Requires later promotion-lifecycle work instead of direct adoption.
effect_hypothesis.expected_risk: Pressure to automate too early may bypass the classifier intent.
effect_hypothesis.effect_metric: Later promotion-lifecycle artifacts can map repair loops back to this improvement entry.

entries.rollback: Return to narrative-only benchmarking if repair-loop borrowing is removed from roadmap scope.

entries.title: Hermes governed skill and memory patterns
entries.summary: Add Hermes-style governed skill and memory mechanisms as selective sources for later lifecycle work.

effect_hypothesis.expected_benefit: Seeds GAP-135 and GAP-136 with explicit external source coverage instead of ad hoc memory growth.
effect_hypothesis.expected_cost: Needs evidence-backed promotion and retirement rules before any live adoption.
effect_hypothesis.expected_risk: Could be confused with immediate runtime support if not tied to later executable contracts.
effect_hypothesis.effect_metric: Future memory and skill lifecycle contracts can trace their source lineage here.

entries.rollback: Remove from the classifier if the repository drops governed skill or memory lifecycle work.

entries.title: Letta memory blocks
entries.summary: Use Letta-style memory-block ideas as a design input, not as an immediate runtime commitment.

effect_hypothesis.expected_benefit: Improves future memory segmentation vocabulary before implementation begins.
effect_hypothesis.expected_cost: Requires later schema and lifecycle work to define actual repository usage.
effect_hypothesis.expected_risk: Block semantics may be overfit if target-repo evidence stays weak.
effect_hypothesis.effect_metric: Memory lifecycle design keeps explicit block-level rationale instead of flat narrative notes.

entries.rollback: Downgrade to narrative research only if no memory-block contract is justified later.

entries.title: Mem0 memory lifecycle
entries.summary: Treat Mem0-style memory lifecycle as an improvement source for evidence-backed retention and pruning behavior.

effect_hypothesis.expected_benefit: Improves future memory retention design with explicit keep, prune, and reactivation intent.
effect_hypothesis.expected_cost: Requires later lifecycle schema and effect metrics work.
effect_hypothesis.expected_risk: Lifecycle language may become more detailed than the current executable surface.
effect_hypothesis.effect_metric: GAP-135 artifacts can express retention and expiry logic using evidence-backed lifecycle fields.

entries.rollback: Remove the lifecycle borrowing if later controlled-evolution scope excludes persistent memory.

entries.title: Aider repo map discipline
entries.summary: Adopt Aider-style repo-map discipline as a governed context artifact rather than an unmanaged helper.

effect_hypothesis.expected_benefit: Prepares GAP-137 to add context-shaping metrics without widening host scope.
effect_hypothesis.expected_cost: Needs governed artifact and measurement fields before rollout.
effect_hypothesis.expected_risk: Repo-map support could bloat context if effect metrics are weak.
effect_hypothesis.effect_metric: Later repo-map artifacts can show token and effect metrics traced to this classifier entry.

entries.rollback: Withdraw the add classification if context shaping remains fully manual.

entries.title: Cline permission surface
entries.summary: Keep permission-surface review as a borrowing source for later tool and credential audit boundaries.

effect_hypothesis.expected_benefit: Preserves a concrete reference for scoped tool and credential controls without adding a new host product.
effect_hypothesis.expected_cost: Requires later audit-boundary typing and proof paths.
effect_hypothesis.expected_risk: Permission semantics may be copied loosely if not normalized in GAP-138.
effect_hypothesis.effect_metric: Audit-boundary design can cite a kept borrowing source with explicit scope.

entries.rollback: Drop the kept source if later audit-boundary work uses only repository-native policy inputs.

entries.title: OpenAI Cookbook and eval patterns
entries.summary: Merge external eval pattern guidance into the repository's own evidence and effect-feedback vocabulary.

effect_hypothesis.expected_benefit: Keeps evaluation language aligned with measurable effect rather than prose-only quality claims.
effect_hypothesis.expected_cost: Requires later unification with repository-native evidence contracts.
effect_hypothesis.expected_risk: Eval terminology may fragment if merge targets stay implicit.
effect_hypothesis.effect_metric: Later certification and effect-harness work can reference merged eval vocabulary.

entries.rollback: Revert to keep or add if merged eval vocabulary becomes too ambiguous for later contracts.

entries.title: Agent Skills governed surface
entries.summary: Add governed skill-surface borrowing as an input to later skill, hook, and eval promotion lifecycle work.

effect_hypothesis.expected_benefit: Creates an explicit source lineage for governed skill promotion instead of implicit borrowing.
effect_hypothesis.expected_cost: Needs later lifecycle rules, evidence, and rollback wiring.
effect_hypothesis.expected_risk: Could be mistaken for direct skill installation authority if not kept subordinate to control packs.
effect_hypothesis.effect_metric: Later lifecycle artifacts can reference a declared external source for governed skills.

entries.rollback: Remove the add classification if skill lifecycle work is reduced to repository-local patterns only.

entries.title: Borrowing matrix research
entries.summary: Keep the borrowing matrix as the upstream review artifact that feeds executable classification.

effect_hypothesis.expected_benefit: Preserves the human review basis for later machine-checkable portfolio entries.
effect_hypothesis.expected_cost: Needs ongoing refresh whenever sources or queue boundaries move.
effect_hypothesis.expected_risk: Matrix drift can weaken later classifier trust if not refreshed with evidence.
effect_hypothesis.effect_metric: Classifier verification can still trace back to the active borrowing matrix.

entries.rollback: Replace with a newer review source only after the successor artifact becomes authoritative.

entries.title: External benchmark notes
entries.summary: Merge older benchmark notes into the classifier-led portfolio instead of leaving them as standalone narrative guidance.

effect_hypothesis.expected_benefit: Reduces narrative-only benchmark sprawl by tying benchmark takeaways to classifier outcomes.
effect_hypothesis.expected_cost: Requires ongoing maintenance when benchmark conclusions change.
effect_hypothesis.expected_risk: Merged notes may lose nuance if not linked back to source research.
effect_hypothesis.effect_metric: Current working set can point to the classifier artifact instead of multiple disconnected benchmark summaries.

entries.rollback: Unmerge by restoring separate benchmark guidance if classifier compression loses needed context.

entries.title: Core principles policy
entries.summary: Keep core principles as a stable kernel input while the classifier only describes later capability decisions.

effect_hypothesis.expected_benefit: Separates stable principles from later capability portfolio changes.
effect_hypothesis.expected_cost: Requires future control packs to map back to principles explicitly.
effect_hypothesis.expected_risk: Portfolio work might accidentally duplicate principle semantics without this separation.
effect_hypothesis.effect_metric: Core-principles verification remains green while classifier work expands around it.

entries.rollback: Revert only the classifier statement if core principles are materially refactored later.

entries.title: Current-source compatibility guard
entries.summary: Keep the compatibility guard as the proof path for host-boundary claims.

effect_hypothesis.expected_benefit: Provides a real verification path for Codex and Claude Code boundary claims.
effect_hypothesis.expected_cost: Needs refresh when source assumptions age out.
effect_hypothesis.expected_risk: Stale compatibility evidence can undermine host-surface decisions.
effect_hypothesis.effect_metric: Compatibility guard output remains usable from docs checks.

entries.rollback: Replace with the newer compatibility source if this guard is superseded.

entries.title: Target repo governance baseline
entries.summary: Keep the target-repo governance baseline as the landing surface that later GAP-134 effect feedback will exercise.

effect_hypothesis.expected_benefit: Preserves the reusable baseline that later effect harness work needs.
effect_hypothesis.expected_cost: Requires inheritance and override verification before effect claims are trusted.
effect_hypothesis.expected_risk: Baseline drift can hide later rollout inconsistencies.
effect_hypothesis.effect_metric: Target-repo governance consistency verification remains green.

entries.rollback: Restore the previous baseline mapping if later rollout contracts change.

entries.title: Runtime evolution review
entries.summary: Improve runtime self-evolution review by adding explicit portfolio decisions instead of proposal-only flow.

effect_hypothesis.expected_benefit: Connects dry-run evolution review output to a governed keep or retire decision model.
effect_hypothesis.expected_cost: Needs later integration work so classifier and evolution artifacts reinforce each other.
effect_hypothesis.expected_risk: Parallel candidate systems may diverge if they stay unlinked.
effect_hypothesis.effect_metric: Classifier and runtime-evolution dry-run flows remain compatible at the evidence level.

entries.rollback: Keep runtime evolution as a separate dry-run lane if convergence proves too costly.

entries.title: AI coding experience extraction
entries.summary: Improve experience extraction so future knowledge and memory lifecycles inherit explicit portfolio decisions.

effect_hypothesis.expected_benefit: Prevents extracted experience from turning into unmanaged long-tail memory.
effect_hypothesis.expected_cost: Requires later lifecycle and promotion wiring.
effect_hypothesis.expected_risk: Experience artifacts may remain informative but not actionable until lifecycle work lands.
effect_hypothesis.effect_metric: Experience review output can be traced to later knowledge and memory queues.

entries.rollback: Leave experience extraction as review-only if no governed lifecycle follows.

entries.title: Claim drift sentinel
entries.summary: Keep the claim-drift sentinel as the proof path that planned work is not misreported as live.

effect_hypothesis.expected_benefit: Protects the GAP-131 queue from accidental overclaiming.
effect_hypothesis.expected_cost: Requires current docs and evidence to stay synchronized.
effect_hypothesis.expected_risk: Out-of-sync status lines can still trigger false positives or missed drift.
effect_hypothesis.effect_metric: Claim-drift sentinel stays green after status and evidence updates.

entries.rollback: Revisit only if a stronger claim-governance mechanism replaces the sentinel.

entries.title: Host feedback surface
entries.summary: Merge host-feedback evidence into the same effect-feedback vocabulary used by the portfolio classifier.

effect_hypothesis.expected_benefit: Lets host feedback participate in the same measurable effect model as later target-repo reuse.
effect_hypothesis.expected_cost: Requires later normalization between operator feedback and repo effect metrics.
effect_hypothesis.expected_risk: Merged feedback streams may stay incomparable until GAP-134 defines the harness.
effect_hypothesis.effect_metric: Host feedback checks still pass while effect-feedback vocabulary expands.

entries.rollback: Keep host feedback separate if merged evidence makes operator signals less clear.

entries.title: Narrative-only borrowing reviews
entries.summary: Deprecate borrowing reviews that stop at prose and never produce machine-checkable effect hypotheses.

effect_hypothesis.expected_benefit: Moves the queue from descriptive research toward executable governance inputs.
effect_hypothesis.expected_cost: Requires a classifier and later contracts to absorb useful narrative content.
effect_hypothesis.expected_risk: Important nuance could be lost if reviews are deprecated without traceability.
effect_hypothesis.effect_metric: Active queue artifacts reference classifier outcomes instead of free-form borrowing prose alone.

entries.rollback: Keep narrative-only reviews active again if machine-checkable outputs become infeasible.
entries.retention_rule: Retain historical research notes, but do not treat them as completion evidence for future gaps.
entries.future_trigger: Stop deprecating only if a future phase explicitly proves narrative review is sufficient for effect certification.

entries.title: Planning-only heavy-stack candidates
entries.summary: Retire large-stack candidate ideas that have no executable control path or funded target-repo effect proof.

effect_hypothesis.expected_benefit: Keeps the queue focused on implementable governed controls instead of speculative platforms.
effect_hypothesis.expected_cost: Some ambitious options remain in history only.
effect_hypothesis.expected_risk: Future readers may overlook retired ideas that become relevant again.
effect_hypothesis.effect_metric: The active queue stays bounded to executable GAP-131 through GAP-139 work.

entries.rollback: Create a new queue item and evidence batch before bringing a retired heavy-stack candidate back.
entries.retention_rule: Keep only as historical planning context, not as an active near-term implementation candidate.
entries.future_trigger: Reopen only if a target-repo effect harness demonstrates a concrete need and funded execution path.

entries.title: Narrative-only capability without effect metric
entries.summary: Delete candidate records that cannot name an effect metric, verification command, or rollback path.

effect_hypothesis.expected_benefit: Removes false progress indicators from the self-evolution backlog.
effect_hypothesis.expected_cost: Requires stricter upfront discipline for future candidate creation.
effect_hypothesis.expected_risk: Potentially useful ideas may be dropped if they are documented too early.
effect_hypothesis.effect_metric: Classifier verification fails closed when required effect fields are missing.

entries.rollback: Restore a deleted candidate only by recreating it with full effect, evidence, and rollback fields.
entries.retention_rule: Do not keep empty candidate shells in the active portfolio.
entries.future_trigger: Allow recreation only after a real effect metric and verification path are known.
